commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P1] Human review is still needed because the code repair is narrow, but proof and the implicit screenshot-to-provider policy are maintainer/contributor gates.; Needs attention: The PR includes good prompt-injection hardening, but it still expands browser screenshot pixels into provider-selected media understanding and needs maintainer acceptance.; Review finding: [P1] Thread agentId through browser screenshot med... (sha=6c27bd48d50ef7f30afafde161ac4c0c394e77a5)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes browser screenshots to request text descriptions through the existing media image-understanding runtime, with sanitized text output and fallback to the original image result.

PR surface: Source +340, Tests +412, Docs +51. Total +803 across 12 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: plugin tool context exposes `agentId`, and media `runCapability` consumes it for image-model/default-provider decisions, but the PR path does not pass it into the browser screenshot media-understanding call. I did not run tests because this was a read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human review is still needed because the code repair is narrow, but proof and the implicit screenshot-to-provider policy are maintainer/contributor gates.

target.title: Feat/browser screenshot vision
target.body:
## Summary

- Reworks browser screenshot vision to use the existing media image-understanding runtime instead of adding browser-specific model config.
- Browser screenshots now ask `describeImageFile` for a text description when the existing image-understanding path can provide one through `tools.media.image`, shared `tools.media.models`, `agents.defaults.imageModel`, or an auth-backed image provider.
- If image understanding skips or fails, the browser tool falls back to the original screenshot image result, so image-capable main models and unconfigured installs keep the old behavior.
- Keeps the hardening on the successful text path: model text is wrapped with `wrapExternalContent`, line-start `MEDIA:` directives are neutralized, and `details.media` is not attached.
- Carries existing media policy into the browser path: screenshot images are sanitized with `agents.defaults.imageMaxDimensionPx` before provider calls, and media scope receives `sessionKey`, channel, and derived `chatType`.

## Behavior / User-visible changes

- No new `browser.*` config fields.
- Existing image-understanding configuration makes browser screenshot descriptions work automatically.
- If the active model already handles image input and media understanding does not choose a separate image model, screenshots continue to return image content.
- The docs now point users at the existing `tools.media.image`, `tools.media.models`, and `agents.defaults.imageModel` settings.

## Security / Policy

- The screenshot image is only sent to the provider selected by the existing media image-understanding runtime.
- Existing media scope policy is evaluated with browser runtime context, including session key, channel, and best-effort chat type.
- Text returned from the image model is treated as untrusted page-derived content. It is wrapped as external content, and `MEDIA:` lines are defanged before the agent media extractor can see them.
- The text-description success path intentionally omits `details.media`, so a described screenshot is not advertised as deliverable channel media.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/browser/index.test.ts extensions/browser/src/browser/vision.test.ts extensions/browser/src/browser-tool.test.ts src/media-understanding/runtime.test.ts`
  - media-understanding shard: 1 file, 21 tests passed
  - browser shard: 3 files, 84 tests passed
- `/Users/steipete/Projects/agent-scripts/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main`
  - clean: no accepted/actionable findings
- PR checks on `6c27bd48d50ef7f30afafde161ac4c0c394e77a5` were passing or still in progress when this body was refreshed; no failed checks were visible.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Text-only main models can receive a text description of browser screenshots through the existing image-understanding pipeline, without a new browser model registry.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout on macOS, focused Vitest coverage for browser tool execution plus media runtime scope handling.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/browser/index.test.ts extensions/browser/src/browser/vision.test.ts extensions/browser/src/browser-tool.test.ts src/media-understanding/runtime.test.ts`.

Evidence after fix: Tests cover configured image-understanding success, no-description fallback, provider-failure image fallback, `MEDIA:` neutralization, `details.media` omission, image sanitization before provider calls, plugin context propagation, derived group chat type, and media runtime `scopeContext`.

Observed result after fix: Browser screenshot success returns wrapped text with provider/model attribution; unavailable or failed image understanding returns the original image result with sanitization preserved.

What was not tested: Live external provider/browser run after the rewrite; the earlier live videos in this PR were from the old `browser.models` design and are no longer the primary proof.
target.author: scotthuang

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
